item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It excretes no waste, but continually loses mass. Shouldn't there at least be a "shit, we dunno" sentence in here?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lo these many years later, and it occurs to me that this is the main course version of the dessert from The Stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or vegetables, grass clippings? Or horns, bones, hide, and other animal byproducts?

Maybe bunnies are just easier to breed and put in the room with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article notes that SCP-1361 can consume any organic matter, including wood, but the containment procedures specify that a mammal weighing between 1-5kg is to be fed to it every day.

Surely it's far more pragmatic to feed it a standardized weight in wood?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So is it safe to eat when it's cooked? Like, are there Foundation BBQs that got way out of hand when it was served? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the first pages I read when i joined. Still enjoy it so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I missed the Aurochs until reading the discussion (and I read the SCP at least twice).That little bit makes this Sooooooo much more interesting! Essentially this seems to have been a grey goo scenario for at least 350 years. That leaves the compelling question of why it didn't eat the world long before the Foundation could contain it (or where it found fresh Aurochs).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing is just a giant Jambonet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hamburger that eats you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not particularly disgusted by it either. The idea just came to me while I was walking down the meat aisle at the store and I said to myself "what if pink slime were alive, and it relied on people eating it to fuel its reproductive cycle?"

I regret nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

…I have to wonder if the fact that I disliked this when so many others liked it is related to the fact that I am not really specially bothered by pink slime. I mean, it's just more meat. >_>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So well done and so, so foul…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm, aurochs meat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, this is so great! It's a vegetarian's nightmare. Amorphous meat product + gelatinous cube!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this is fucking gross.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's safe because we have a pretty good understanding of its behavior and because it's of minimal threat as long as we follow proper containment procedures. As long as we keep it locked up and don't let it grow so big that its pheremones start seeping out into the complex, it can't do anything to hurt us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't understand classifications, it is Safe because it can be contained easily and it is highly predictive. Euclid applies to objects that are a little harder to contain but only because they are random or unpredictable. Keter is extremely hard to contain and once escaped is extremely dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SAFE? WTF? This should be Keter or Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But then we can't have a Foundation Shoggoth Rumble, where all the amorphous SCPs fight each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We don't destroy things for the sake of destroying them, but if we ever need to destroy something, we'd want to know how and if we can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course and I'm not saying that the Foundation would ever just start chucking SCPs into this thing but there is no reason they would not test how this thing handles other SCP tissues, if this thing could destroy 682 then they would test it, same for any other specimens marked for destruction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this thing probably could digest topsoil and plastic, but you're correct that that was not my intention.

Cheerfully adjusted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
682 is pretty much the only example. To quote from the cliche list:

We're the Special CONTAINMENT Procedures, not Special DESTRUKTION Procedures Foundation. We usually dun destroy things just to destroy them nowadays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682 is a prime example of "if we can destroy this thing then we will try every means available to us".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They might not be able to destroy it if something goes wrong.

That, and they're testing to see if things can be destroyed, which is in general against the Foundation's mission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You do realize that "organic" doesn't mean "biological", right?

Otherwise why isn't this thing devouring all the topsoil and plastic with which it comes in contact?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is hilarious. Honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has the potential to go horribly wrong, sure, but I can't imagine that the Foundation's reaction to an organism that eats everything wouldn't be to see if they could feed dangerous SCP animals to it, especially since they know they can still destroy the meat-blob if it goes wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it's cross-testing SCPs. That has the potential to go horribly wrong, and I don't see the Foundation doing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's orange slime.

Processed everything (now with ammonia hydroxide!)

The Ultimate Mystery Meat.

And yet, for all the fun I can poke at this thing, it's well-written and otherwise great. This sentence:

When not interfered with by outside stimuli, SCP-1361 will move in a straight line until it encounters a barrier larger than its current height, at which point it will begin moving in a different direction.

kind of serves no purpose. It basically says, "It goes until it stops and then it goes somewhere else." There's definitely a better way to express its constant state of aimless locomotion. +1 though. I think I just like the inherent weirdness of "But it smells so good!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does anyone else think that "Pink Slime Shoggoth" would be a great name for a band?

As for the article… congrats, you've found a way to make mechanically separated meat creepier, which let me tell you is no mean feat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did pretty much start out with "pink slime that's alive" as the basis for this one. I think the hardest part was finding a picture that looked menacing enough to be the basis for an SCP.

Re cooked SCP-1361, the effect is the same, it still consumes you providing you eat enough of it. It probably takes longer and requires you to eat more due to the damage done by the cooking process.

Re SCP-682; I actually thought up exactly how that termination log would go, but I didn't want to post it until I saw whether this was going to go over. Now posted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually really liked this.

Two minor points.

1. The effects of eating cooked SCP-1361 aren't mentioned, and even if they're negligible or non-existent I think the article would benefit from a line or two describing them.

2. Full stops after weights aren't required, and I found this a little grating.

At any rate, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682 would rip out it's own guts before this thing could do any real damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I were to hazard a guess, I'd say that this little snippet makes this thing just a bit overpowered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious as to the SCP-682 termination log addiion this could become.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going in, I thought this was going to be too much like one of my old SCPs, SCP-157, but this is a different enough take on the idea that I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…and samples of organic SCPs not known to be destructible by conventional means.

Downvoted here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cannot possibly upvote this, but the fact that I am not downvoting it is a testament to your skill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bwah. Horrid, revolting and disgusting. All in the best of ways. And of course my mind went to "What happens if we get a huge blob of this glop and dump it on 682?" Well 682 would probably tear-ass out of there, and/or spawn some form of non-organic outer shell out of nowhere. So, yeah, that's out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a pink slime shoggoth. I am okay with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ewww. A pink slime amoeba. I found this disgusting in an entertaining way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hahahaha. awesome and gross. great work, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ewwwwww

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's really convincing, and I liked how you injected the scary factor into the junk food/mystery meat thing and the "you are what you eat" thing. Upvoted! You sure are prolific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this! +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They don't touch it and get absorbed into it, per se - they eat a chunk of it, and it consumes them from the inside and forms a copy of itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good, however there is one bit that isn't sitting right for me. The fact that humans get a compulsion to touch it and get absorbed into it is a bit too much like The Big Ball O' Tits, and dilutes a really interesting SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sort of nuetral on the concept. Not my type of SCP, but not bad at all.

I went +1 because of "wet [EXPLETIVE DELETED]". Hearing that made my day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn
On one side, this is a DnD monster.
On other side , the picture is win.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is this a pink slime joke

because that was my first thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of two food-related SCPs i've had kicking around in my head for awhile and I'm very interested to see how it's going to go over.
